mnemonics.title: Queue Operations Order
mnemonics.content: FIFO: First In, First Out - Remember: People queue at ticket counter, first to come gets ticket first.

mnemonics.title: Enqueue vs Dequeue
mnemonics.content: ENqueue adds to END (rear), DEqueue removes from BEGINNING (front). Think: 'E' for Enter/End, 'D' for Depart/Beginning.

mnemonics.title: Queue vs Stack
mnemonics.content: Queue = FIFO (First In First Out), Stack = LIFO (Last In First Out). Remember: Queue has 'ueue' sounding like 'queue' (line of people).

acronyms.usage: Principle followed by queue data structure where first element added is first to be removed.
Principle followed by stack data structure, opposite of queue.
Graph traversal algorithm that uses queue to explore nodes level by level.
Uses queue (especially priority queue) for task scheduling.
Position where new elements are added (enqueue operation).

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a horizontal line where people stand. New people join at the RIGHT end, and people are served from the LEFT end. The line moves forward when someone leaves.
Think of a round merry-go-round. When you reach the end, you simply go back to the start and continue. The front and rear chase each other in a circle.
Add new person at the END of the ticket line. The rear pointer moves forward to show where the next person will stand.
Person at the FRONT gets served and leaves. The front pointer moves to the next person in line.

analogies.analogy: A ticket counter line or queue at a bank. People join at the back and are served from the front in order.
A round dining table where guests keep coming and leaving - seats get reused as people leave and new ones arrive.
Emergency room in a hospital - patients are treated based on severity (priority) rather than arrival time.
A double-door hallway where people can enter and exit from both ends.
